Name: Remove Tag from an Attribute
Author: admin
Description: Remove tags from attributes in MISP.

DynamicResults.ResultExample: [{"name": "Tag unique___test(7) successfully removed from Attribute(9).", "message": "Tag unique___test(7) successfully removed from Attribute(9).", "url": "/tags/removeTagFromObject"}]

Parameters.Description: Specify a comma-separated list of attribute identifiers from which you want to remove tags. Note: If both “Attribute Name“ and “Attribute UUID“ are specified, action will work with “Attribute UUID“ values.
Specify the ID or UUID of the event, where to search for attributes. This parameter is required, if “Attribute Search“ is set to “Provided Event“ or Object UUID is provided.
Specify a comma-separated list of tags that you want to remove from attributes.

Specify a comma-separated list of categories. If specified, action will only remove tags from attributes that have matching category. If nothing is specified, action will ignore categories in attributes. Possible values: External Analysis, Payload Delivery, Artifacts Dropped, Payload Installation.

Specify a comma-separated list of attribute types. If specified, action will only remove tags from attributes that have matching attribute type. If nothing is specified, action will ignore types in attributes. Example values: md5, sha1, ip-src, ip-dst
Specify the UUID of the object that contains the desired attribute.

Specify a comma-separated list of attribute UUIDs from which you want to remove new tags. Note: If both “Attribute Name“ and “Attribute UUID“ are specified, action will work with “Attribute UUID“ values.

Specify, where action should search for attributes. If “Provided Event“ is selected, action will only search for attributes or attribute UUIDs in event with ID/UUID provided in “Event ID“ parameter. If “All Events“, action will search for attributes among all events and remove tags from all attributes that match our criteria.

AIDescription:
Removes specified tags from attributes within the MISP platform. This action allows users to target attributes based on their names, UUIDs, categories, or types. The search for these attributes can be scoped to a specific MISP event or performed across all events in the system. 

### Parameters
| Parameter | Type | Mandatory | Description |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Tag Name | String | Yes | A comma-separated list of tags to be removed from the identified attributes. |
| Attribute Name | String | No | A comma-separated list of attribute identifiers (values) to target. |
| Attribute UUID | String | No | A comma-separated list of attribute UUIDs to target. If both Name and UUID are provided, UUID takes precedence. |
| Event ID | String | No | The ID or UUID of the MISP event. Required if 'Attribute Search' is set to 'Provided Event' or if an 'Object UUID' is specified. |
| Attribute Search | Dropdown | No | Determines the search scope: 'Provided Event' (default) searches within the specified Event ID, while 'All Events' searches globally. |
| Category | String | No | A comma-separated list of MISP categories (e.g., 'Payload delivery', 'Network activity') to filter the attributes. |
| Type | String | No | A comma-separated list of MISP attribute types (e.g., 'md5', 'ip-src') to filter the attributes. |
| Object UUID | String | No | The UUID of a specific MISP object. If provided, the action only targets attributes belonging to this object. |

### Additional Notes
- If 'Attribute Search' is set to 'Provided Event', the 'Event ID' parameter must be populated.
- If an 'Object UUID' is provided, the 'Event ID' must also be provided to locate the object.

### Flow Description
1. **Initialization**: The action parses the input parameters and establishes a connection to the MISP API.
2. **Validation**: It verifies that mandatory parameters are present and that conditional requirements (like Event ID for specific search scopes) are met.
3. **Tag Resolution**: It fetches the internal MISP identifiers for the provided tag names to ensure they exist.
4. **Attribute Discovery**: Based on the search scope (Global or Event-specific) and filters (Name, UUID, Category, Type, Object), it retrieves the list of matching attributes from MISP.
5. **Tag Removal**: For every matching attribute found, the action sends a request to MISP to remove each of the specified tags.
6. **Reporting**: The action summarizes which attributes had tags successfully removed and which failed or were not found.
